### 280-kids-echo-canyon
**Status**: `demoable` В· **Cycle shipped**: 290 В· **Last touched**: 2026-06-03

Open `/dream/280-kids-echo-canyon`. **For kids (4+).** Press **start singing**, then sing, hum, or call across the canyon вҖ” a little paper creature ("Echo") on the far cliff catches your phrase and sings it right back as a flight of colored paper birds, then layers a gentle harmony underneath. The lab's **first call-and-response / canon piece**: everything else in the lab reacts to sound or generates it; this one *listens to you, waits, and answers* вҖ” the oldest musical game there is, turned into a toy a four-year-old can play alone.

Four subsystems: (1) **mic + pitch detection** вҖ” `getUserMedia` вҶ’ `AnalyserNode` (analysis only; the voice is never played back, recorded, or sent), and ~every 130 ms a frame of time-domain samples runs through normalized **autocorrelation** pitch detection (Chris Wilson's canonical Web Audio method, YIN family, parabolic-interpolation refined); (2) **phrase segmentation** вҖ” an RMS gate captures degrees as you sing and ~620 ms of quiet ends the phrase, octave-collapsed so a high or low child voice both land in the same comfortable register; (3) **echo + harmony scheduler** вҖ” Echo replays your phrase note by note on a soft triangle mallet and adds a diatonic **third** 150 ms behind each note for a round-like shimmer, over an always-on C+G drone through a `DynamicsCompressor` limiter (safe sounds, never silent); (4) **cut-paper canyon render** вҖ” rAF/refs-only, birds fly sine-arc beziers cliff to cliff, the singing creature bobs and opens its beak, color = pitch.

Built straight at the 2026-06-02 JURY's three demands: **non-pentatonic** (the scale is **C-Lydian** вҖ” its raised 4th floats, deliberately not the C-major pentatonic the lab kept defaulting to; no wrong notes вҖ” anything you sing is snapped into the mode), **non-luminous** (pure matte **cut-paper on Canvas2D** вҖ” flat dusk sky, paper cliffs with drop-shadow lips, a paper moon, birds as colored ovals; pure `source-over`, drop-shadows only, **no glow, no additive, no WebGL**), and it **audits the sound** (the mode is the point). **Input = mic** вҖ” chosen because `touch` is at Г—4 in the last-10 window (260/264/268/272) and therefore banned this cycle; mic was under-represented and is loved (`158-kids-hum-paint`вқӨпёҸ). Degrades gracefully: no mic / permission denied вҶ’ a **self-playing demo** where the two creatures sing call-and-response to each other on a timer (readable `text-rose-300` invite to allow the mic), so it's always demoable. No reading, no fail states, вүҘ64px start button, ~14-min calm session. No API route, no `guard` needed (fully client-side; no server, no secrets).

Serves KIDS.md's explicitly-named **vocalization** goal (call-and-response is the purest singing prompt for 4yo language + music development) and the calm/contemplative corner KIDS.md stakes out. References: **YIN** (de CheveignГ© & Kawahara, JASA 2002), **Chris Wilson PitchDetect** (`cwilso/PitchDetect`; the lab already uses Wilson's two-clocks in `256`), **Pauline Oliveros, *Deep Listening*** (2005). Born from RESEARCH В§290. Zero new deps В· zero API.

Design notes: `src/app/dream/280-kids-echo-canyon/README.md`

### 287-mirror-choir
**Status**: `demoable` В· **Cycle shipped**: 295 В· **Last touched**: 2026-06-03

Open `/dream/287-mirror-choir`. Press **Begin**, allow the camera, and stand back вҖ” your whole body becomes a choir. This is the lab's **first body-tracking piece** (MediaPipe Pose Landmarker, 33 real-time 3D landmarks loaded at runtime from a CDN, no npm dep) and its **first vocal-formant synthesis** (Klatt-style: a sawtooth glottal pulse split through three parallel bandpass formants F1/F2/F3 per voice). Your left and right hands are two singing voices whose pitch follows a warm **D-Dorian** chord stack by their height in frame; **arm openness** (wrist-span Г· shoulder-span) morphs the vowel continuously ooвҶ’ohвҶ’ehвҶ’ah; **body height** shifts the register. Two softer pad voices + a low sine drone keep a chord under it all, through a delay tail вҖ” so it's never silent and never atonal (anything you do lands in the mode).

Deliberately **non-glow**: a matte **"wooden mirror"** (Daniel Rozin, *Wooden Mirror* 1999) вҖ” the camera is sampled into a grid of 14px tiles that light warm amber where your landmark-hull silhouette + pixel-brightness fall and stay near-black elsewhere; pure Canvas2D `source-over`, drop-shadows only, no additive/WebGL. Degrades gracefully: no camera / permission denied / MediaPipe fails вҶ’ a **"ghost dancer"** loops 7 hand-authored keyframe poses (smooth-stepped over 20s) that drive the exact same choir + mirror, with a readable `text-rose-300` notice вҖ” **fully demoable at 06:30 on a phone with no camera**. The mic is never touched (camera-only; nothing recorded). No API route, no `guard` needed (camera + CDN model + Web Audio are all client-side; no server, no secrets). Zero new deps.

**First MediaPipe / body-tracking + first formant synthesis in the lab** вҖ” the ambition floor's own example of a never-used technique. Born from RESEARCH В§295. Shipped as the winner of a **WIDE** 3-explorer adult fire across three empty shelves; siblings `aurora-wire` (live NOAA space-weather sonification вҖ” the lab's 2nd real-world-data source) + `still-room` (eyes-closed HRTF spatial audio navigated by phone tilt вҖ” first non-screen + first DeviceOrientation controller) build-verified and banked in IDEAS.md.

Design notes: `src/app/dream/287-mirror-choir/README.md`

### 290-kids-sound-safari
**Status**: `demoable` В· **Cycle shipped**: 296 В· **Last touched**: 2026-06-03

Open `/dream/290-kids-sound-safari`. **For kids (4+).** Press the big **Start** button, then turn your whole body in a slow circle вҖ” six animals (frog, bird, whale, cricket, owl, bee) are hidden at fixed bearings *around* you in 3D sound, each humming a soft motif. When you turn to **face** one, its song swells to front-and-center and brightens, the phone gives a tiny `navigator.vibrate` buzz, the animal blooms big onto the mostly-dark screen and sings a happy phrase вҖ” "found!" вҖ” and joins the found-row. Find all six вҶ’ they sing a chord together, then it gently resets and loops. No reading, no fail, no timer; headphones make it magical, speakers still convey left/right.

This is the lab's **first deliberately non-screen / audio-FIRST piece** and its **first DeviceOrientation (heading) controller** in a shipped prototype вҖ” the screen is a footnote (a faint warm dusk-meadow compass + the found-row); the experience lives in spatial sound moving around the listener's head. Built straight at the 2026-06-03 JURY's provocations #2 (kids must *move* their body, not poke вҖ” touch input was banned this cycle), #3 (ship the spatial breadth banked-but-never-shipped вҖ” this is the `still-room` concept finally realized, recast for kids), #4 (build the unused head-tracked-spatial half of RESEARCH В§295), and #5 (output is spatial *audio*, not a screen visualizer).

Subsystems: (1) **HRTF binaural panning** вҖ” one continuous `PannerNode` (`panningModel="HRTF"`, inverse distance) per animal at its compass bearing, plus an always-on ambient pad so it's never silent; (2) **DeviceOrientation head-tracking** вҖ” `alpha` rotates the `AudioListener` forward vector (feature-detects modern `forwardX/Z` AudioParams vs legacy `setOrientation`; same modern-vs-legacy detection for the panners), with iOS `requestPermission()` inside the click handler; (3) **find/collect state engine** вҖ” per-frame nearest-bearing test (within 25В°) вҶ’ gain swell + lowpass brighten + bloom; first-find fires the sung phrase + vibrate + found-row; all-found вҶ’ celebration chord вҶ’ gentle auto-reset; (4) **minimal warm Canvas2D compass** render (no WebGL/three.js, no glow). Degrades gracefully: if no `deviceorientation` events arrive within ~2s (desktop / denied / no sensor), it falls back to **pointer-drag + вҶҗ вҶ’ arrow keys + a slow hands-free auto-tour** that finds the animals by itself with a readable `text-rose-300` hint вҖ” **fully demoable at 06:30 on a phone with zero sensors**. Mic is never opened; no recording. No API route, no `guard` needed (Web Audio + a motion sensor are all client-side; no server, no secrets). Zero new deps.

References: **Pauline Oliveros, *Deep Listening*** (2005); **Bernhard Leitner, *Sound Space*** (sound you move through); the **2026 head-tracked-spatial-audio wave** (THX Spatial Audio+ AI head tracking, CES Jan 2026 вҖ” the browser-feasible equivalent is `PannerNode` HRTF + DeviceOrientation, zero hardware). Born from RESEARCH В§296. Shipped as the winner of a **WIDE** 3-explorer kids fire; siblings `kids-shadow-dance` (camera frame-difference dancing вҶ’ blooming meadow) + `kids-sky-band` (today's real Open-Meteo weather вҶ’ a generative kids band) build-reviewed and banked in IDEAS.md.

Design notes: `src/app/dream/290-kids-sound-safari/README.md`

### 291-harmonograph
**Status**: `demoable` В· **Cycle shipped**: 297 В· **Cycle 2 (expressive)**: 299 В· **Cycle 3 (polychrome)**: 301 В· **Last touched**: 2026-06-04

**Cycle 3 (cycle 301) вҖ” the polychrome specimen.** The multi-cycle thread reaches its planned 3rd cycle. The figure is no longer one color: **each held note draws its OWN running-composite thread in its circle-of-fifths color** (`pitchClassToColor`: `hue=((pc*7)%12)/12`, s 0.78 v 1.0 вҶ’ `hsvToRgb`), so a major triad visibly **weaves from three distinct-but-kindred hues** вҖ” and because a fifth is one constant hue step, the chord reads as a single family rather than three clashing colors (RESEARCH В§301 chose circle-of-fifths over the naive chromatic-rainbow mapping for exactly this reason). New `sampleCompositeUpTo` sums pendulums `[0..i]` but normalizes by the FULL set's amplitude so partial threads stay spatially registered with the full figure; it still cleans up under Pure tuning because every composite is built from the same JI-snapped ratios. Added the lab's **first vector export**: **Export SVG** emits one aspect-corrected `<polyline>` per colored thread over a dark ground вҶ’ `harmonograph-<chord>.svg`, a true printable specimen (the PNG raster export from cycle 2 stays). A color **legend** mirrors the drawn threads (swatch + note name). References: **Chord Colourizer** (arXiv 2510.10173, 2025 вҖ” CQT chord вҶ’ Newton's color wheel), **Jack Ox**'s circle-of-fifths color/harmony wheel, **maddie lim**'s "12 Tone Color Theory." Build 9.29вҶ’**10.4 kB**. Shipped as the winner of a **DEEP** 2-approach fire; the parallel **scope-mode** explorer (continuous PureвҮ„Equal XY phosphor vector-scope + beat-Hz/LOCKED readouts + presets, folding in the banked `phase-scope`, citing Lissajous/Jerobeam Fenderson/Ryoji Ikeda) is build-verified and banked in IDEAS.md as **cycle 4**. Build-verified, not browser-verified вҖ” unverified surface: multi-strip draw legibility on a dense chord, the SVG aspect projection, PNG readback on Safari.

**Cycle 2 (cycle 299) вҖ” the expressive live instrument.** The lab's first multi-cycle thread advances instead of starting fresh. Four performance layers now sculpt the figure as it draws, each with a MIDI control AND a no-hardware fallback: **sustain pedal вҶ’ figure-HOLD/accrete** (MIDI CC64 / Space bar / on-screen pad вҖ” released notes keep ringing AND keep contributing their decaying pendulum so the figure *accretes* as you layer chords over a held bass; lifting drops them from audio + figure together; HUD shows held-vs-pedaled, on-screen keys tint when pedaled); **mod-wheel вҶ’ pendulum damping** (MIDI CC1 / вҶ‘вҶ“ keys / slider вҶ’ exponential decay multiplier `0.35В·17^d`, loose-sprawl вҶ’ tight-spiral, with a readable `NN%` + word label); **velocity вҶ’ ink intensity** (avg held velocity drives a `uInk` shader uniform so dynamics are visible); **PNG export** (`preserveDrawingBuffer:true` + `canvas.toBlob` вҶ’ `harmonograph-<chord>.png`). Engine gained `HarmonographSynth.setPedal()` (parks key-released voices, returns dropped midis on release); the render loop composites held + pedaled notes. Build-verified (build size 7.51вҶ’**9.29 kB**), not browser-verified вҖ” unverified surface: pedal accrete edge cases (re-strike a parked note) and PNG readback on Safari (see STATE В§299). Shipped as the winner of a **DEEP** 2-approach fire; the parallel `harmonograph-spectrum` explorer (per-note Newton-color-wheel threads + SVG vector specimen export, citing Chord Colourizer arXiv 2510.10173) is build-verified and banked in IDEAS.md as **cycle 3**.

Open `/dream/291-harmonograph`. Press **Start sound**, then play a chord вҖ” on a **MIDI keyboard**, your **computer keys** (`a w s e d f t g y h u j k o l p ;`, `z`/`x` = octave), or the **on-screen piano** вҖ” and the harmony *draws itself* as a Victorian **harmonograph**: each held note becomes a decaying pendulum whose frequency ratio is taken against the lowest held note, traced as `x(t)=ОЈ aбөўВ·sin(rбөўt+ПҶбөў)e^(вҲ’dбөўt)`, `y(t)=ОЈ aбөўВ·cos(rбөўt+ПҶбөў+kбөў)e^(вҲ’dбөўt)`. A prominent **Pure tuning (Just Intonation)** toggle snaps every ratio to the nearest small-integer just interval for BOTH the oscillator pitch AND the pendulum rates вҖ” so a consonant chord's figure visibly **cleans up into a near-closed spirograph** while the audible beating settles, at the same instant; under 12-TET the irrational ratios make the curve drift and tangle. That coupling вҖ” *see it close, hear it settle* вҖ” is the whole point.

Four subsystems: (1) **three-way note input вҶ’ one note-on/off path** вҖ” Web MIDI (`requestMIDIAccess({sysex:false})`, `onstatechange` hotplug, device-name readout), auto-repeat-guarded QWERTY, and a 2-octave on-screen piano (вүҘ44px, multi-touch pointer events); (2) **warm 12-voice Web Audio synth** (sine + +7Вў detuned triangle вҶ’ lowpass вҶ’ ADSR вҶ’ shared feedback delay вҶ’ master вҶ’ `DynamicsCompressor` limiter, velocity вҶ’ loud+bright, soft always-on drone); (3) **JI-lock + chord/ratio analysis** (live HUD: held notes, best-guess chord name, active ratio set e.g. `1/1 : 5/4 : 3/2`); (4) **raw WebGL2 renderer** (NOT three.js, NOT Canvas2D) вҖ” hand-written GLSL ES 3.00, VAO/VBO, ~3000-point `LINE_STRIP` `bufferSubData`'d each frame, additive glow, translucent fade-quad ink trail, idle Lissajous "seed" figure when nothing is held, DPR/resize-aware. Optional **MIDI-out echo** (off by default) forwards held notes to the first output port, so the output isn't only a screen.

This is the lab's **first harmonograph / harmony-as-visible-geometry** piece, and it claims the **AMBITION-MANDATE criterion #4 (multi-cycle commitment) for the first time in the lab's history** вҖ” the 2026-06-03 JURY's #1 provocation (zero multi-cycle builds in 15+ cycles, "stop shipping orphans"). It is the banked, build-verified `281-midi-harmonograph` breadth resurrected (JURY provocation #3: "ship the breadth you've already paid for instead of citing new breadth"), and it inverts the audioвҶ’visual driver the JURY flagged (#5: output a MIDI/sound instrument, not a soundвҶ’picture viz). **Novelty honesty**: Web MIDI itself already exists at `4-operator` вҖ” the novel technique is the **harmonograph geometry** (0 prior hits in INDEX/READMEs, grep-verified), not MIDI. Diversity: INPUT=MIDI/keyboard В· OUTPUT=raw-WebGL2 line-geometry В· TECHNIQUE=harmonograph+JI В· VIBE=theory-literate live instrument вҖ” dodges the JURY's banned [touch В· three.js В· poke-toy-in-exotic-tuning] AND the over-represented [matte-Canvas2D 5Г—]. Degrades gracefully: no Web MIDI (e.g. Safari) вҶ’ amber notice + working keyboards; MIDI-but-no-device вҶ’ amber prompt; no WebGL2 вҶ’ rose notice (audio + keyboards still work). No API route, no `guard` needed (Web Audio + Web MIDI + WebGL2 all client-side; no mic, no recording, no network, no secrets). Zero new deps. References: the **harmonograph** (Hugh Blackburn pendulum apparatus, ~1840s) + **Lissajous figures** (J. A. Lissajous, 1857). Shipped as the winner of a **DEEP** 2-explorer adult fire; sibling **`phase-scope`** (a continuous PureвҮ„Equal XY oscilloscope tuning-lock вҖ” see+hear consonance settle on three channels at once) build-verified and banked in IDEAS.md. **Multi-cycle thread вҖ” cycle 2 (299) ADDED**: sustain-pedal figure-hold/accrete, mod-wheelвҶ’pendulum-damping, velocityвҶ’ink, PNG export (see top of this entry). **Cycle 3 will add**: per-note Newton-color-wheel threads (circle-of-fifths hue) + SVG vector specimen export + color legend (banked in IDEAS.md from the parallel explorer).

Design notes: `src/app/dream/291-harmonograph/README.md`

### 298-kids-echo-friend
**Status**: `demoable` В· **Cycle shipped**: 302 В· **Last touched**: 2026-06-04

Open `/dream/298-kids-echo-friend`. **For kids (4+).** Press the big **"Sing to me вңЁ"** button, then **sing any little phrase** вҖ” a soft glowing creature *listens*, **sings your phrase back** in its own warm voice, and **remembers** it. Every few rounds it plays a growing **little song** made of everything you've sung together. No reading, no timer, no score, no fail; the creature hums a gentle drone so it's never silent.

This is the kids lane's first **call-and-response with memory** вҖ” the antidote to the lane's local minimum ("poke a cute thing вҶ’ it drones, no memory"). It reframes the banked `294-kids-voice-garden` **out of the C-pentatonic rut** (quantizes to **D-Dorian**) and moves `296-kids-firefly-tilt`'s remember-and-replay mechanic onto a **fresher input** (voice, not tilt) вҖ” exactly the two queued kids moves from STATE В§301.

Four subsystems into one pipeline: (1) **real-time monophonic pitch detection** вҖ” YIN/NSDF (normalized square difference) autocorrelation on raw `AnalyserNode` PCM, no ML, no CDN model, mic analysis-only (never recorded/uploaded), each detected Hz snapped to D-Dorian; (2) **call-and-response memory engine** вҖ” collects a phrase frame-by-frame, detects ~0.8вҖ“1.2 s of silence as phrase-end, dedupes into notes, pushes to a growing memory, and every 3rd phrase replays the whole accumulated song; (3) **warm sing-back synth** вҖ” sine + triangle through lowpass + short delay, drone-duck while echoing, all through a `DynamicsCompressor` limiter so it can never get loud/harsh; (4) **hand-written WebGL2 GLSL** creature вҖ” an SDF blob with domain-warped organic jiggle on an aurora ground, pitch-driven hue (violetвҶ’amberвҶ’tealвҶ’lime across the Dorian range), one orbiting **memory orb per remembered phrase**, and a sing-back pulse ring. Degrades gracefully: **no mic / permission denied вҶ’ a hands-free auto-demo** sings 6 hand-authored D-Dorian phrases through the *identical* pitchвҶ’echoвҶ’memory pipeline (so the whole loop demos itself on a phone with no mic), with a readable `text-rose-300` notice; **no WebGL2 вҶ’ rose notice, audio still runs**; iOS-safe (mic + AudioContext created inside the click handler). No API route, no `guard` needed (Web Audio + WebGL2 are client-side; no recording, no network, no secrets). Zero new deps.

References: **SingingSDS** (arXiv:2511.20972, Nov 2025 вҖ” a dialogue agent that *responds by singing*; this is its fully-client, no-AI, kid-sized embodiment), **Pauline Oliveros, *Deep Listening*** (the listen-then-answer tradition), and the classic **call-and-response / "Simon"** memory game. Born from RESEARCH В§302. Shipped as the winner of a **WIDE** 3-explorer kids fire; siblings `299-kids-clap-band` (clap вҶ’ onset-detection layered groove, Steve Reich *Clapping Music*) + `300-kids-blow-sail` (blow вҶ’ breath-envelope sailing, Wiener-entropy breath/tone discriminator) build-reviewed and banked in IDEAS.md. **Build-verified, not browser-verified** вҖ” unverified surface: whether YIN pitch-tracking on a real 4-year-old's wobbly voice lands cleanly on the Dorian grid, and whether the sing-back-and-grow loop reads as a "song we're building together" vs. a list of echoes.

Design notes: `src/app/dream/298-kids-echo-friend/README.md`

### 303-kids-wind-harp
**Status**: `demoable` В· **Cycle shipped**: 304 В· **Last touched**: 2026-06-04

Open `/dream/303-kids-wind-harp`. **For kids (4+).** Hit the big amber **"Tilt to play в–ё"** button, then **tilt your phone or iPad** вҖ” gravity swings a row of seven glowing strings like a wind-harp, and any string that swings far enough **plucks itself and sings**. The child plays music by *tipping the world*, hands-free; no tapping, no reading, no timer, no fail.

The lab's **first tilt-input piece since the loved `83-kids-tilt-rain`**, and a deliberate pull on three loved prototypes (`83-kids-tilt-rain`вқӨпёҸ tilt В· `105-pluck-field`вқӨпёҸ Karplus pluck В· `140-kids-string-bridge`вқӨпёҸ playable strings). It couples two genuinely **fresh-for-this-lab techniques**: (1) **Verlet-integration string physics** вҖ” each of 7 strings is a 14-node mass-chain solved with Verlet + distance-constraint relaxation (3 passes/frame) on a **fixed 120 Hz step**; the device-tilt `deviceorientation` ОІ/Оі becomes a **gravity vector**, so tipping the tablet changes which way "down" is and the hanging strings swing and settle with believable physical motion; (2) **Karplus-Strong plucked-string synthesis** (Karplus & Strong, 1983) вҖ” when a string's midpoint swing crosses a threshold it plucks: a noise burst rendered offline through a tuned lowpass-fed delay line into an `AudioBuffer`, bigger swing вҶ’ louder/brighter/longer, per-string refractory so it never machine-guns, stereo-panned lowвҶ’high. A third subsystem is the hand-written **raw WebGL2** ribbon renderer (matte alpha-over string curves expanded along their normals on a soft breathing dark gradient, a glow flash per pluck вҖ” **no additive bloom, no three.js**). The 7 strings are tuned to **D-Dorian** (D E F G A B C вҖ” explicitly NOT C-major-pentatonic), with an ambient D+A drone underneath and a `DynamicsCompressor` limiter so simultaneous plucks can never blast.

Degrades at every step so it's **fully playable on a desktop with no sensors**: iOS 13+ `DeviceOrientationEvent.requestPermission()` is called inside the Start gesture (denial вҶ’ readable `text-rose-300` note + fallback); **pointer-drag** tilts the gravity vector on desktop; and a **2 s auto-sway** kicks in when no tilt events arrive so the harp **plays itself** and a 4-year-old (or Karel at 06:30 on his phone) always sees causeвҶ’effect. No API route, no `guard` needed (Web Audio + WebGL2 all client-side; no mic, no camera, no network, no secrets). Zero new deps.

References: the **Aeolian harp** (a stringed instrument played by moving air rather than fingers вҖ” here the child's tilt is the wind); the **Karplus-Strong** plucked-string algorithm (1983). Chains from RESEARCH В§304 (*Rhythm in the Air*, arXiv:2511.00793, Nov 2025 вҖ” embodied motion вҶ’ scale-constrained musical output; tilt is the body, D-Dorian is the scale constraint). Winner of a **WIDE** 3-explorer kids fire; siblings `299-kids-clap-band` (clap вҶ’ HFC-onset-detection layered groove, Steve Reich *Clapping Music*) + `300-kids-blow-sail` (blow вҶ’ Wiener-entropy breath-envelope sailing) build-reviewed and re-banked in IDEAS.md. **Ambition 2/5**: never-used-technique(#1, Verlet rope physics) + named-reference(#3). **Build-verified, not browser-verified** вҖ” unverified surface: the deviceorientationвҶ’gravity *feel* and pluck threshold on a real tilting hand, the iOS permission branches, and the rendered look of the ribbon strips (no sensors/GPU in this sandbox).

Design notes: `src/app/dream/303-kids-wind-harp/README.md`

### 306-kids-rain-shaker
**Status**: `demoable` В· **Cycle shipped**: 306 В· **Last touched**: 2026-06-04

Open `/dream/306-kids-rain-shaker`. **For kids (4+).** Tap the big amber **"Shake to play"** button, then **shake the phone or iPad like a rainstick or maraca** вҖ” gentle shakes make a soft trickle of beads, bigger shakes tumble a warm rain down the screen, and each strong shake-peak strikes a **D-Dorian** bell. The child plays by *moving the whole device*, hands-free; no tapping, no reading, no timer, no fail. An always-on pad keeps it alive, and a `DynamicsCompressor` brick-wall limiter means even the most vigorous shaking can never blast.

The lab's **first `devicemotion` / accelerometer instrument** in 300+ prototypes вҖ” and deliberately a *different sensor and axis* from the lab's prior motion pieces: `303-kids-wind-harp` and `290-kids-sound-safari` used `deviceorientation` (tilt ОІ/Оі and compass heading Оұ вҖ” device *orientation*), whereas this reads `accelerationIncludingGravity` for shake *energy*. Three subsystems: (1) **`shake.ts`** вҖ” per `devicemotion` event, read the acceleration vector, subtract a slow per-axis running average (a **high-pass** that removes the constant gravity component), take the magnitude, and feed a smoothed **shake-energy envelope** (fast attack, slow release); threshold crossings with a ~130 ms refractory emit discrete **hit** events while the continuous envelope drives bead density. Every input path (motion, pointer-shake, auto-demo) feeds the IDENTICAL `pushSample` pipeline. (2) **`rain-audio.ts`** вҖ” a soft D-Dorian pad drone (detuned triangles, breathing lowpass) + a rainstick trickle of short filtered-noise bead grains whose spawn rate/brightness track the live energy + **FM bell chimes** struck on hit events (panned, velocity from hit strength, lowpassed so they never get piercing), all summed through a final `DynamicsCompressor` used as a limiter. (3) **`rain-gl.ts`** вҖ” raw **WebGL2** (hand-written GLSL ES 3.00): a soft **darkвҶ’dawn** gradient quad that warms with energy + a CPU-simulated pool of up to 600 falling **bead/rain particles** as soft point-sprites in **matte premultiplied alpha-over** (the lab's non-additive house style вҖ” switched from the builder's additive blend during curation to honor the JURY's anti-glow ban) + warm glow blooms on each bell. DPR/resize aware.

Degrades for the 06:30 phone review: iOS 13+ `DeviceMotionEvent.requestPermission()` is called inside the Start tap (denial вҶ’ readable `text-rose-300` note + fallback); on a sensor-less device it falls back to **pointer/mouse-shake** (fast pointer movement synthesises an acceleration vector into the same detector) and a gentle **auto-demo** that rains and chimes by itself; no WebGL2 вҶ’ `text-rose-300` notice, audio still runs. `AudioContext` is created inside the Start tap (iOS-safe). No API route, no `guard` needed (Web Audio + a motion sensor + WebGL2 are all client-side; no mic, no camera, no network, no secrets). Zero new deps.

References: the **rainstick** (Andean/Chilean cactus-spine instrument played by tilting and shaking) + the **maraca / shaker** percussion tradition; the embodied-motionвҶ’sound mapping echoes movement-sonification research (**CHI 2026**, *Designing Interactive Movement Sonification*). Born from RESEARCH В§306. Winner of a **WIDE** 3-explorer kids fire; siblings `304-kids-clap-band` (clap вҶ’ HFC-onset-detection layered groove, Steve Reich *Clapping Music*, D-Mixolydian) + `305-kids-blow-sail` (blow вҶ’ Wiener-entropy breath-envelope sailing, C-Lydian) build-verified and re-banked in IDEAS.md. **Ambition 3/5**: never-used-technique(#1, devicemotion shake-energy) + named-reference(#3) + recent-research(#5, В§306). **Build-verified, not browser-verified** вҖ” unverified surface: the shake threshold/scale *feel* on a real shaking hand (one-number fixes if too touchy or too stiff), the auto-demo chime cadence, and WebGL2 point-size behaviour across GPUs (no phone sensor / GPU in this sandbox).

Design notes: `src/app/dream/306-kids-rain-shaker/README.md`

### 371-kids-clap-along
**Status**: `demoable` В· **Cycle shipped**: 336 В· **Last touched**: 2026-06-07

Open `/dream/371-kids-clap-along`. **For kids (4+).** Tap the big amber **"Clap to play в–ё"** button, then **clap your real hands** вҖ” a friendly creature claps a short rhythm, you clap it back, and **the phone HEARS your claps through the microphone** (no screen-tapping). On a good-enough match the creature delights and the shared rhythm **grows by one clap** вҖ” Simon-style вҖ” so the little song the two of you share gets longer and longer. No reading, no score, no fail; a missed clap-back just warmly replays the same pattern ("let's try together").

The lab's **first prototype that actually LISTENS for a real hand-clap.** A grep audit found the lab has shipped call-and-response/echo rhythm **five times** (`154-kids-clap-back`, `213-kids-echo-drum`, `209-kids-drum-tap`, `150-kids-beat-builder`, `218-kids-xylophone-drops`) вҖ” and **every one is TOUCH** (the device plays a pattern, the child taps the screen back). None hear a real clap; onset detection itself was banked-but-never-shipped (`299`/`304-kids-clap-band`). 371 finally does it. Four subsystems: (1) **`onset.ts`** вҖ” real-time **acoustic onset detection**: each animation frame it reads the mic's FFT from an `AnalyserNode` (`getFloatFrequencyData`, `fftSize` 1024, `smoothingTimeConstant` 0 so transients aren't blurred), computes a **spectral-flux Г— high-frequency-content (HFC)** novelty (half-wave-rectified positive bin-to-bin energy increases, weighted toward high bins so a bright broadband clap pops while the steady drone/voice stays low), compares it to an **adaptive threshold** (slow running mean Г— slack + floor, so it self-tunes to a loud kitchen or quiet bedroom), and applies a **~140 ms refractory window** so one clap вҖ” even a wobbly toddler double-bounce вҖ” counts once. (2) **`pattern.ts`** вҖ” a **growing-memory call-and-response state machine** (`intro вҶ’ calling вҶ’ waiting вҶ’ judging вҶ’ celebrate`): the creature calls a rhythm, the child's detected onset times are greedily matched against the target beats within a generous **Вұ0.34 s** tolerance, ~60% lands = a match вҶ’ celebrate + **grow the pattern by one beat** (occasionally a syncopation); a miss costs nothing. The growing shared rhythm IS the song вҖ” the **memory/consequence** the jury asked the kids lane for after `350-kids-bump-along` regressed to a memory-less poke-toy. (3) **`clap-audio.ts`** вҖ” an always-on soft **D + A drone** + **D-Dorian** tuned clap voices (D E F G A B C вҖ” explicitly NOT C-major-pentatonic), call panned/brightened one way and the child's answer the other (two voices), beats tuned up the scale by position so the growing rhythm reads as a little tune, a rising reward sparkle on each grow, all through a `DynamicsCompressor` hard limiter so it can never blast. (4) **`creature-gl.ts`** вҖ” raw **WebGL2** (hand-written GLSL ES 3.00): one warm **breathing creature** (squashes when it claps, leans in to listen on the child's turn, eyes curve into happy arcs when delighted) above a **row of glowing clap beads** that grows with the song so the child can *see* it getting longer вҖ” amber for the call, mint for the answer вҖ” in **matte premultiplied alpha-over** (the lab's non-additive house style, no glow-stacking).

One unified pipeline, three doors: every input route is judged by the **identical** onset machine вҖ” **mic** (real acoustic claps via `sampleMic()`), **pointer/tap clap** (mic denied вҶ’ a tap plays a **real broadband noise burst** into the *same* analyser so it's detected exactly like a hand clap), and **auto-demo** (no claps for ~4.5 s вҶ’ the creature calls AND claps its own answer back through the same `clap()вҶ’analyserвҶ’detector` path, so the piece **plays itself hands-free** for the 06:30 phone review). Degrades at every step: `AudioContext` + `getUserMedia({audio})` created **inside the Start tap** (iOS-safe); mic is analysis-only (never recorded/uploaded, analyser not routed to the speakers вҶ’ no feedback); mic denied вҶ’ readable `text-rose-300` notice + tap-to-clap fallback; no WebGL2 вҶ’ `text-rose-300` notice, audio still runs. No API route, no `guard` needed (mic-analysis + WebGL2 all client-side; no network, no secrets). Zero new deps.

References: **Drumball** (Audio Mostly 2024 вҖ” a tangible call-and-response drumming system inspired by the West African djembe) В· **Steve Reich, *Clapping Music* (1972)** В· the African call-and-response / talking-drum tradition. Born from RESEARCH В§336 (lab-gap audit: 5Г— clap-back, all touch; 0Г— real mic clap). Winner of a **WIDE** 3-explorer kids fire; siblings `372-kids-blow-garden` (blow вҶ’ Wiener-entropy breath-envelope growing garden) + `373-kids-firefly-path` (tap-to-lay-stones вҶ’ firefly replays a growing D-Dorian path) build-reviewed and banked in IDEAS В§336. **Ambition 3/5**: never-used-technique(#1, acoustic clap onset detection) + вүҘ3-subsystems(#2) + named-reference(#3). **Build-verified, not browser-verified** вҖ” unverified surface: the onset thresholds/HFC weighting on a real mic (too-hot self-trigger vs. too-deaf soft claps; defaults lean forgiving), drone-vs-mic self-noise under real AGC, the Вұ0.34 s / ~60% match tolerance against a real toddler clap-back, and the WebGL2 creature/bead sizing (no mic / GPU in this sandbox).

Design notes: `src/app/dream/371-kids-clap-along/README.md`

### 375-tempo-canon
**Status**: `demoable` В· **Cycle shipped**: 337 В· **Last touched**: 2026-06-07

Open `/dream/375-tempo-canon`. **The accompanist that follows YOU.** Press the big violet **"Play demo в–ё"** button (or play it yourself on the home-row keys / a Web MIDI keyboard): you play the melody of Beethoven's **Ode to Joy** (the right hand), and the system aligns your live performance to the reference score in real time and plays the **bass + chord accompaniment (the left hand) locked to your tempo** вҖ” speeding up when you rush, slowing when you ritard. The signature is that you can *literally see the machine following you*: the **warping path** вҖ” a glowing diagonal that bends steeper when you rush and flattens when you drag вҖ” is the centerpiece visual.

The JURY's **#1 standing adult ask, finally taken**: a real-time score-follower / live-accompaniment tool. Score-following had **lost curation three times** (re-rejected cycle 335) for three specific reasons, and this build is a deliberate answer to each: (a) *"not lab-first вҖ” `26-score-follow` exists"* вҶ’ this is the live **accompanist** (it *plays the accompaniment in sync with your rubato*, a different deliverable from `26`'s bare cursor), and its **online-DTW warping-path render is genuinely lab-first** (grep: no prior prototype draws an alignment path); (b) *"needs mic INPUT, which the JURY bans"* вҶ’ it is driven by **MIDI / computer-keyboard / a baked demo, NO microphone**, dodging the ban entirely; (c) *"real-instrument verifiability wall a sandbox can't cross вҖ” the thing that killed it 3Г—"* вҶ’ solved exactly the way `358-beat-mirror` solved it, with a **built-in known performance** (see below) that self-proves the whole pipeline with zero hardware. The JURY's own provocation #1 prescribed precisely this ("solve it the way 358 did, with a known internal answer").

Four subsystems: (1) **`dtw.ts`** вҖ” **online / streaming Dynamic Time Warping** (Simon Dixon's MATCH lineage, 2005): instead of a full NГ—M offline matrix it grows the alignment path one note at a time inside a **bounded search window** (`radius 5`) ahead of the last committed column, via the classic 3-predecessor recurrence `D(i,j)=cost(i,j)+min(D(i-1,j),D(i,j-1),D(i-1,j-1))`, keeping only the current/previous frontier rows; commits are **forward-only** (position never jumps backward) so the accompaniment can't stutter. **Local tempo = the slope of the warping path** (steeper than 45В° = rushing, shallower = dragging). (2) **input layer** вҖ” one `handlePlayedNote` pipe fed by three doors: the baked **demo**, **computer keyboard** (home-row `a s d f g h j k` вҶ’ D-major scale, labels shown), and **Web MIDI** (`navigator.requestMIDIAccess()`, graceful status if absent вҖ” never throws). (3) **`audio.ts`** вҖ” Web Audio synthesis only: a detuned-oscillator piano voice with a fast-attack/expo-decay env; an optional soft **melody echo** + the star **accompaniment** (functional bass + chord triad in D major, I/V/vi/IV) whose chord decay scales with the slope-derived tempo, so the left hand rings longer when you drag. (4) **`gl.ts`** вҖ” raw **WebGL2** with custom shaders plotting reference-score-time (X) Г— your-performance-time (Y): the glowing **bending warping path**, the faint **45В° in-step guide**, the translucent **bounded search window** band, a pulsing **current-cell cursor**, and expanding **accompaniment fire-rings**, one per chord.

The headline verifiability feature is the **built-in known performance** (`makePerformance()` in `score.ts`): a baked Ode-to-Joy with expressive rubato вҖ” a steady intro, an **accelerando** through the middle, a **ritardando** into the final held note, plus deterministic per-note timing jitter вҖ” emitted note-by-note on wall-clock timers. The follower never sees the score positions; it only receives pitches over time and must align them. So pressing one button proves the whole pipeline **with no microphone, no MIDI device, no camera**: you watch the warping path bend (steepening in the accelerando, flattening in the ritard), the BPM readout swing, and the accompaniment lock on. Degrades cleanly: no WebGL2 вҶ’ readable `text-rose-300` notice, follower + audio still run. No API route, no `guard` needed (Web Audio + MIDI + WebGL2 all client-side; no mic, no network, no secrets). Zero new deps.

References: **Matchmaker вҖ” *An Open-source Library for Real-time Piano Score Following and Systematic Evaluation*** (arXiv:2510.10087, Oct 2025; systematically compares online-DTW vs HMM followers) В· **Simon Dixon, *Live Tracking of Musical Performances Using On-Line Time Warping* / MATCH** (2005). Born from RESEARCH В§337. Winner of a **DEEP** 3-approach adult fire (one concept, three follower algorithms); siblings `374-the-accompanist` (HMM/forward note-event follower + confidence meter, score-river viz) + `376-the-prompter` (cue/anchor FSM robust to wrong notes, build-time-proven 8/8 cues) build-verified and banked in IDEAS В§337 as the winner's **next-cycle deepenings**. **Ambition 3/5**: вүҘ3-subsystems(#2, input + online-DTW follower + Web-Audio elastic accompaniment + WebGL2 warping-path) + named-reference(#3, Matchmaker/Dixon) + multi-cycle-commitment(#4, "The Accompanist" thread вҖ” cycle-1 ships alignment+warping-path+self-verifying demo; cycle-2 folds in the HMM/confidence follower behind a DTW/HMM toggle + ACCompanion-style expressive dynamics following; cycle-3 maps Karel's own *Welcome Home* melody as the score). **Build-verified, not browser-verified** вҖ” unverified surface: whether a real fumbly performance keeps the forward-only DTW aligned (a long string of wrong notes could outrun the window-5 search before re-locking), the slopeвҶ’tempo blend feel on real rubato, the warping-path legibility at a 06:30 phone glance, and real Web MIDI on hardware (no MIDI/GPU in this sandbox).

Design notes: `src/app/dream/375-tempo-canon/README.md`

### 380-expressive-accompanist
**Status**: `demoable` В· **Cycle shipped**: 339 В· **Last touched**: 2026-06-07

Open `/dream/380-expressive-accompanist`. **The accompanist that follows your EXPRESSION, not just your tempo.** Press the big violet **"Play demo в–ё"** (or play the home-row keys / a Web MIDI keyboard): you play the melody (right hand) of **Pachelbel's Canon in D**, and the system plays the **bass + chord accompaniment (left hand)** that follows you in **three dimensions at once** вҖ” it speeds/slows with your *tempo*, swells/fades with your *dynamics* (play soft вҶ’ it answers soft), and detaches/sustains with your *articulation* (tap staccato вҶ’ its chords go short and crisp; hold legato вҶ’ they ring). Cycle 1 of this thread (`375-tempo-canon`) followed only your tempo; this is the deepening that makes the machine feel like a duet partner instead of a backing track.

**Cycle 2 of the lab's "Accompanist" multi-cycle thread**, and the most direct realization of **The ACCompanion**'s central thesis (Cancino-ChacГіn/Peter/Widmer, IJCAI 2023): *tempo alone sounds mechanical вҖ” following dynamics and articulation is what makes an accompanist human.* The lab-first axis is **expressive-parameter coupling**: a grep of every prior README shows no prototype that *follows a soloist's dynamics or articulation* (375 follows only tempo). Three subsystems past the input layer: (1) **`dtw.ts`** вҖ” online/streaming **DTW** (Simon Dixon's MATCH, 2005): a bounded forward-only warping path (radius вүҲ5, 3-predecessor recurrence `D(i,j)=cost+min(D(i-1,j),D(i,j-1),D(i-1,j-1))`, frontier rows only) gives *which* score position you're at and a local-tempo slope вҖ” extended in cycle 2 with two EMA trackers: `smoothedVelocity` (Оұ 0.35) and `smoothedArticulation` = note-held-duration Г· inter-onset-interval (Оұ 0.30; ~1 = legato, ~0 = staccato). (2) **`audio.ts`** вҖ” Web Audio synthesis only: a detuned-oscillator piano melody echo + the star **accompaniment** (functional bass + chord triads, Pachelbel ground bass I-V-vi-III-IV-I-IV-V) whose **loudness scales with the followed dynamics**, **decay length scales with the followed articulation**, and **chord placement is driven by the DTW tempo**, all summed through a `DynamicsCompressor` brick-wall limiter so it can never blast. (3) **`gl.ts`** вҖ” raw **WebGL2** (hand-written GLSL ES 3.00): the cycle-1 warping path, now wearing an **expression ribbon** вҖ” a halo layer whose **point-sprite size encodes live dynamics** (fat glow = loud) and whose **screen-space dashing (`u_dash` uniform) encodes articulation** (solid = legato, dotted = staccato), over a solid cyan-white alignment core; plus a translucent search-window band, a 45В° in-step guide, a pulsing committed-cell cursor, and expanding **fire-rings** on each chord whose size = that chord's loudness. DPR/resize aware.

One unified `handlePlayedNote(pitch, velocity, durationMs)` pipe, three doors: the baked **demo** (default; auto-plays ~2 s after load so the 06:30 phone reviewer sees + hears all three couplings hands-free), **computer keyboard** (home-row `a s d f g h j k` вҶ’ a D-major scale, labels shown; hold for legato, tap for staccato), and **Web MIDI** (`navigator.requestMIDIAccess()`, graceful status if absent вҖ” never throws; real velocity drives dynamics). The headline verifiability move (the thing that killed score-following 3Г— before `375` solved it the `358-beat-mirror` way): the baked **"known performance"** (`makePerformance()` in `score.ts`) bakes deliberately wide, *legible* swings in all three axes вҖ” a steady intro вҶ’ **accelerando** вҶ’ big **ritardando**; a **crescendo** (ppвҶ’ff) then **diminuendo** (ffвҶ’mp); a **legato** first half (ratio ~0.85) then a **staccato** second half (ratio ~0.25). The follower never sees score positions вҖ” only `(pitch, velocity, durationMs, dt)` tuples вҖ” so pressing one button proves the whole tempo+dynamics+articulation pipeline **with no MIDI, no mic, no camera**: the ribbon visibly swells then thins, dashes then re-solidifies, and the accompaniment audibly swells/fades and detaches/sustains to match. Degrades cleanly: no WebGL2 вҶ’ readable `text-rose-300` notice, follower + audio still run; no Web MIDI вҶ’ status line, keyboard + demo still work; `AudioContext` created inside the Play tap (autoplay-safe, silent catch). No API route, no `guard` needed (Web Audio + MIDI + WebGL2 all client-side; no mic, no network, no secrets). Zero new deps.

References: **The ACCompanion** вҖ” Cancino-ChacГіn, Peter & Widmer, *Combining Reactivity, Robustness, and Musical Expressivity in an Automatic Piano Accompanist* (IJCAI 2023, arXiv:2304.12939; the expressive-coupling north star) В· **Peransformer** вҖ” score-aware-discriminator expressive performance rendering (arXiv:2510.10175, Oct 2025) В· **Simon Dixon, MATCH** online-DTW (2005). Born from RESEARCH В§339. Winner of a **DEEP** 3-approach adult fire (one concept вҖ” *an accompanist that follows your expression* вҖ” three technical attacks); siblings `381-resilient-accompanist` (dual DTWвҮ„HMM follower with confidence-gated handoff that survives wrong/skipped notes) + `382-anticipating-accompanist` (Kalman-style predictive tempo model that schedules the accompaniment *onto your predicted next onset* so it plays *with* you, not behind) build-verified and banked in IDEAS В§339 as the winner's **cycle-3 deepenings** (fold robustness + anticipation into the expressive follower). **Ambition 3/5**: never-used-technique(#1, expressive-parameter following вҖ” dynamics + articulation, lab-first) + named-reference(#3, ACCompanion/Peransformer/Dixon) + multi-cycle-commitment(#4, "The Accompanist" thread cycle 2; cycle-3 folds in 381's robustness + 382's anticipation; cycle-4 maps Karel's own *Welcome Home* melody as the score). **Build-verified, not browser-verified** вҖ” unverified surface: whether the EMA articulation ratio reads cleanly off real (vs baked) playing, the dynamics/decay coupling *feel* through the limiter when chords overlap, the `u_dash` staccato dashing + velocity-sized fire-rings render as intended across GPUs, and real Web MIDI velocity on hardware (no MIDI/GPU in this sandbox).

Design notes: `src/app/dream/380-expressive-accompanist/README.md`

### 384-kids-firefly-chorus
**Status**: `demoable` В· **Cycle shipped**: 340 В· **Last touched**: 2026-06-07 В· **For**: kids (4+)

Open `/dream/384-kids-firefly-chorus`. **Tip the world to gather scattered fireflies until they spontaneously blink вҖ” and sing вҖ” in unison.** A dark dusk meadow holds ~280 fireflies, each blinking out of phase and humming its own slightly-different soft note (a gentle twinkly chaos). **Tilt the device** (or drag a finger; or just watch) to drift fireflies together вҖ” and when they cluster they begin to *nudge each other's blink timing* until a gathered group locks into a single pulsing blink and its scattered pitches collapse onto one shared D-Dorian chord. The child *feels chaos becoming harmony* by gathering them; clusters that have synced stay synced. No "wrong", no fail, no score вҖ” a meadow that breathes into unison.

The lab-first axis is **spontaneous synchronization** itself: a grep of every prior README shows fireflies as a decorative motif (`122-kids-firefly-song`, `211-kids-firefly-web`, `188-kids-glow-bug`) but **no prototype implements coupled-oscillator phase-locking** вҖ” the *emergence* of order from disorder has never been the mechanic. Three subsystems: (1) **`kuramoto.ts`** вҖ” a genuine **Kuramoto model**: each firefly is a phase oscillator integrating `dОёбөў/dt = Пүбөў + (K/Nбөў)В·ОЈвұј sin(Оёвұј вҲ’ Оёбөў)` summed over **only its neighbours** within a coupling radius (cheap **spatial-hash grid**, 3Г—3 cell window, ~280 oscillators at 60fps), flashing when Оё crosses 2ПҖ; **union-find** clusters the locked groups; it computes both a **per-firefly local order parameter** and the **global Kuramoto order parameter rвҲҲ[0,1]** (`rВ·e^{iПҲ} = (1/N)В·ОЈ e^{iОёвұј}`), surfaced as a legible "togetherness" meter (rвүҲ0 chaos вҶ’ rвүҲ1 unison) вҖ” the synchronization made *visible*, in the legible/instructional register the jury asked the lab to feed. (2) **`audio.ts`** вҖ” Web Audio only: an always-on soft D+A summer-night pad bed (never silent) + **D-Dorian** sine bells (D E F G A B C вҖ” explicitly **not** C-major-pentatonic) that, as a neighbourhood's local r rises, blend toward the shared **D-F-A tonic triad**; a 14-voice pool + per-firefly refractory + probability gate so hundreds of flashes never machine-gun; a low-pass tone and a `DynamicsCompressor` brick-wall limiter so it can never blast; a 12-min goodnight fade. (3) **`gl.ts`** вҖ” raw **WebGL2** (GLSL ES 3.00): a dusk-indigo meadow gradient (warm vignette tracks global r) + instanced soft-glow firefly quads in **matte premultiplied alpha-over** (the lab's non-additive house style), DPR/resize aware.

One sim, three input doors, all converging on the same model: **device tilt** (`DeviceOrientationEvent`, iOS `requestPermission()` requested inside the Start tap) вҶ’ a gravity vector that drifts the fireflies; a **pointer-drag** "breeze" fallback that drifts them identically when orientation is denied/absent; and an **auto-demo** вҖ” after ~3s with no input a slow synthetic wandering breeze herds the meadow into one cluster, so the 06:30 phone reviewer *sees the blinks lock and the togetherness meter climb and hears the chord settle* without touching anything or needing a sensor. Degrades cleanly: orientation denied вҶ’ readable `text-rose-300` notice + drag fallback + auto-demo still runs; no WebGL2 вҶ’ `text-rose-300` notice (audio still runs); `AudioContext` created inside the Start tap (autoplay-safe). No API route, no `guard` needed (DeviceOrientation + WebGL2 + Web Audio all client-side; no mic, no camera, no network, no secrets). Zero new deps.

References: **Yoshiki Kuramoto (1975)** вҖ” the coupled-phase-oscillator model В· **Christiaan Huygens (1665)** вҖ” the original "sympathy of clocks" coupled-pendulum synchronization В· the spontaneous flash-synchrony of **SE-Asian *Pteroptyx* fireflies** В· live research front: **arXiv:2603.08352** ("Synchronization of higher-dimensional Kuramoto oscillators on networks," March 2026). Born from RESEARCH В§340. Winner of a **WIDE** 3-explorer kids fire (three unrelated emergent-system sound worlds, each dodging the jury's banned tags вҖ” touch / mic-voice / inline-SVG); siblings `383-kids-coral-garden` (shake вҶ’ **Diffusion-Limited Aggregation** coral growth, WittenвҖ“Sander 1981) + `385-kids-ant-garden` (wave at camera вҶ’ **ant-colony stigmergy** pheromone-trail song, Deneubourg 1990 / Dorigo 1992) build-reviewed and banked in IDEAS В§340 вҖ” both grep-verified lab-first techniques. **Ambition 3/5**: never-used-technique(#1, coupled-oscillator synchronization) + named-reference(#3, Kuramoto/Huygens) + recent-research(#5, arXiv:2603.08352, Mar 2026). **Build-verified, not browser-verified** вҖ” unverified surface: the coupling constant K (3.4) + radius (0.13) feel on a real cluster (too strong вҶ’ instant lock reads as no-emergence; too weak вҶ’ never syncs), the tiltвҶ’gravity mapping on a real tilting hand, whether the sync reads as *magic* vs. a glitch at a 06:30 phone glance, and the voice-pool load when many neighbourhoods flash at once (no sensor/GPU in this sandbox).

Design notes: `src/app/dream/384-kids-firefly-chorus/README.md`
